commit.message:
fix(tool-guard): fix emoji corruption in generated PowerShell hooks

ConvertTo-Json on Windows does not escape non-ASCII to \\uXXXX by
default, causing the warning emoji (U+26A0 U+FE0F) to render as
'??' in many console environments.

Changes applied to copilot-cli.md and claude-code.md PS1 templates:
- Add [Console]::OutputEncoding = UTF8 / \System.Text.UTF8Encoding+UTF8EncodingSealed = UTF8 header
- Replace literal emoji strings with \⚠\️ var
- Add -EscapeHandling EscapeNonAscii to ConvertTo-Json (PS 7.1+)

Also added a generation rule to SKILL.md and a note to README.md so
future consumers know why char codes are used and don't 'fix' it back.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [autofix.ci] apply automated fixes

* fix: restore includeEmptyStacks=true pre-creation behavior in processStackFile

The original monolithic ExecuteDescribeStacks pre-created finalStacksMap[stackFileName]={components:{}}
for every processed stack, so that with includeEmptyStacks=true the result contained both:
- logical stack names (e.g., tenant1-ue2-dev) with actual component data
- raw file-name keys (e.g., orgs/cp/tenant1/dev/us-east-2) with empty components

The refactored code only created entries in processComponentEntry, so import-only stacks
(and the filename-keyed empty counterpart of stacks with components) were never added to
finalStacksMap. Both calls returned 10 stacks regardless of includeEmptyStacks.

Fix: in processStackFile, when p.includeEmptyStacks=true, pre-create under initialName
(manifest name if set, else stackFileName) before the componentsSection check.
filterEmptyFinalStacks removes these empty entries when includeEmptyStacks=false.

Extend trail blur, flat stroke, pigment mix, and impasto to all brush types (#31)

* Initial plan

* feat: Add Gaussian Blur Trails, Kubelka-Munk Pigment Mixing, and Heightmap Impasto

Co-authored-by: gigigugit <113522248+gigigugit@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gigigugit/boid_brush/sessions/d557270d-a165-44e9-b9c0-88579a90d253

* fix: limit trail blur to current stroke only via per-stroke accumulation canvas

Co-authored-by: gigigugit <113522248+gigigugit@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gigigugit/boid_brush/sessions/f9ed3201-d83a-4174-816a-d8c7b411cb24

* fix: BoidBrush paint at first start by doing initial stamp in onDown

The boid brush relied entirely on onFrame() (called from requestAnimationFrame)
to produce stamps. On a quick tap (pointerdown + pointerup before the next RAF
frame), isDrawing goes false before onFrame runs, so no stamps are deposited and
no paint is visible. Other brushes (SimpleBrush, BristleBrush) stamp in
onDown/onMove and flush compositing in onUp, so they always work.

Fix: perform one initial simulation step and stamp agents in onDown so paint
appears immediately on every stroke, including quick taps. Also flush any
pending compositing in onUp (matches SimpleBrush.onUp._flushComposite pattern).

Co-authored-by: gigigugit <113522248+gigigugit@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gigigugit/boid_brush/sessions/fbbd8e54-9205-4cc8-b038-18ddb145563a

* feat: extend trail blur, flat stroke, pigment mix, impasto to all brush types

UI: Remove data-brushes restrictions from Trail Blur, Pigment Mix, Impasto sections
(now visible for all brushes). Move Flat Stroke from boid-only Spawn section to
the shared Stamp section.

BristleBrush: Add flat stroke (snapshot/composite) and trail blur (blur accum
canvas) support. _stampBristles gains flat/blurCtx params; onFrame and taperFrame
use flat ctx when active.

SimpleBrush: Add flat stroke and trail blur support. _stamp routes to _strokeCtx
when flat and accumulates in blur canvas. _flushComposite handles both composite
and blur application.

EraserBrush: Override onDown forces _flatActive=false (flat stroke not meaningful
for destination-out composite).

commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design

Default SSH sessions to compact (focus) mode on first open (#266)

Change `readStoredValue()` in `use-focus-mode.ts` to return `true` when
the localStorage key is absent, so new users land directly in focus mode
(fullscreen terminal with compact controls) without any manual toggle.

Users who have previously opted out (`localStorage` contains `"false"`)
are unaffected. The toggle continues to persist the explicit choice on
every click.

Update `SshSessionDetails` frontend tests:
- Add `localStorage.setItem("ralpher-ssh-focus-mode", "false")` to
`beforeEach` so tests that don't exercise focus mode see the normal view
and can interact with the header/session-info as before.
- Rewrite the four focus-mode-specific tests to start from the true
default (key absent → focus mode ON) by removing the override at the top
of each test, and adjust expectations accordingly.

feat: index title + og:description for richer semantic embeddings

Previously only the article title was embedded. Now each document is
indexed as 'Title. Description' using the og:description meta tag,
which contains a meaningful summary of the article content.

This significantly improves semantic search recall — queries about
concepts mentioned only in the article body (not the title) now match
correctly. Existing indexes should be rebuilt with build-index.

Also includes entity decoding for meta tag content (& etc.) and
expanded Range header from 8KB to 16KB to capture longer <head> sections.
